name: Escaliers peak
description:
This course offers an experience suspended above the enchanted forest of Iraty. Short and accessible to all, the route includes peaks which offer spectacular views of the Pyrenees mountains.

The largest beech forest in Europe, the Iraty forest is one of the jewels of the Basque Country. Its 20,000 hectares are spread between France and Spain and bring together many remarkable plant and animal species: beeches, firs, deer, roe deer, etc. The route begins in the heart of the thousand-year-old massif, at the Iraty chalets, on the Bagargiak pass. This starting point is ideal since it includes parking, a restaurant, a shop and even offers the possibility of sleeping there.

When you leave the forest, an uphill section awaits you. This leads to the summit of Arthanolatze, the highest point of the outing. A few hundred meters further, you reach the Pic des Escaliers. These two peaks offer their share of panoramas, dizzying and infinite, while overlooking the majestic Iraty forest.

The last part of the route takes a short descent towards the forest. From then on, you only have a short climb left before returning to your starting point, at the chalets.
tips: The Iraty chalets are located at Bagargiak Pass at 1,327 meters above sea level. Attached to the village of Larrau, the chalets are located in the beautiful Iraty forest as well as on the GR 10 trail. There you will find something to eat and stock up on water.
The Arthanolatze culminates at 1,531 meters above sea level. It forms a unique belvedere that offers spectacular views of Soule and Basse-Navare in the Basque Country.

The Pic des Escaliers opens up a beautiful panorama of Soule and Basse-Navarre. A path on a broad ridge joins the Arthanolatze. It offers spectacular views: on one side the immense Iraty forest and on the other the Orhy peak, the first 2,000 meters of the Pyrenees.
